🚨 Why You Should NOT Fall for the HSBC x Apple 6X Offer – A Contrarian View

Before you rush to grab the deal (or worse, give in to FOMO 😅), here are a few things worth considering.

👉 The Offer: HSBC is offering 6X Reward Points on Apple purchases made through the HSBC-Unicorn online portal till 31st July

🛒 Portal: https://exclusiverewards.unicornstore.in

⚠️ The offer is NOT applicable on regular Unicorn stores or the generic Unicorn website.

💳 Eligible Cards:
✅ HSBC Prive
✅ HSBC Premier
✅ HSBC Visa Platinum
✅ HSBC RuPay Platinum

❌ HSBC Travel One Card is not eligible.

Now, let’s talk about the deal breakers👇

👉 This offer makes meaningful sense only for Premier and Prive cardholders. For Platinum cards, the value-back isn’t very exciting.

😄 And let’s not even discuss the Prive Card because 99.9999% of credit card users aren’t eligible for it.

👉 HSBC Premier Cardholders earn 18 Reward Points per ₹100 spent, which can translate into:

🏨40% value-back when redeemed on Accor

✈️ Around 18% value-back is relatively easy even through other redemption options, including HSBC’s 1 Point = ₹1 on flights/hotels.

✅✅✅ 40% value-back is massive. No doubt about that.

👉 Bonus points are capped at 12k Points per calendar month

📌 This means you can purchase ₹1.33 lakh worth of Apple products in June and another ₹1.33 lakh in July and still earn full bonus points

⚠️ HSBC will consider the settlement date for reward capping.

So:

🔸 Purchases on 29th-30th June may get counted in July

🔸 Purchases on 30th-31st July may miss the offer if settlement happens after the offer period

👉 Better to complete purchases well before last date

⏳ Bonus Reward Points will be credited within 45 days.

⚠️ If you are counting on the recently announced HSBC-to-Accor transfer bonus promotion, there is a possibility that the promotion may come and go before bonus points are credited. The last Accor transfer bonus offer lasted only for 5 days.

📢 Rumours of HSBC discontinuing Accor transfers caused many people to empty their HSBC points balances. Just a day later, HSBC announced an Accor transfer bonus offer.

🤔 HSBC may not want customers to enjoy both the Apple offer and a future Accor transfer bonus on the same spend.

Now let’s talk about Apple pricing on HSBC Unicorn vs other platforms 💰

👉 Pricing on HSBC Unicorn is reasonable and not highly inflated. Still, it’s possible to get 5-10% real savings elsewhere through more Instant discount and other card offers.

If I had to compare cash savings and Accor points value, I would discount the value of Accor points by at least 30%.

That means, if I am saving ₹70 in real cash vs ₹100 worth of Accor points, I would go for the real cash savings.

If you are not an Accor fan like me, discount the rupee value of Accor points even more.

💵 Remember, cash is king. Accor points can only be used for Accor hotels, while cash can be used for Accor as well as all other kinds of spending.

Basis the above principle, compare your savings.

And don’t forget the massive upcoming festive sale season 🎉

🗓️ Flipkart Big Billion Days and Amazon Great Indian Festival sales are expected on 11th October.

By then:

📱 iPhone 18 would likely be launched.

📉 Current-generation iPhones could already see a 10-15% price correction.

🎯 And then comes the massive festive sale discount

For context:

🔥 In Big Billion Days 2025, 

iPhone 16 Price dropped from ₹72k to ₹48k. ➡️ 33% savings

📱 iPhone 16 Pro Price dropped from ~₹1.1 lakh to ₹70k ➡️ 39% savings

🔥 In Big Billion Days 2021, I got an iPhone 12 at ₹44k vs a pre-sale price of ₹68k. ➡️ 35% real savings

The point?

🤷High possibility that Apple products see 35-40% discounts during festive sales from current price

If you can wait 4 months, getting 40% value in Accor points today may not necessarily be the best deal.

🚫 Assess your need and urgency, and then decide. Don’t fall for FOMO.

👉SpendWisely Deals Telegram Channel: https://t.me/spendwiselydeals

✈️ Another insane value of Air India Maharaja Club Miles 🤩😱

💰 1 Air India Maharaja Club Mile = ₹3.13

Just booked 3 Economy Class tickets from Mumbai to my hometown - Udaipur for 12,000 Miles + ₹2,466 taxes.

The same flight tickets (even the lowest-cost Value Saver fares) are costing ₹39,996. 😲

So effectively, 12,000 miles saved ₹37,530, which means:

🔥 1 Maharaja Club Mile = ₹3.13

⚠️Yes, air-miles based bookings are more complicated and come with restrictions in terms of award seat availability as compared to revenue bookings or flat-value bookings (e.g. through SmartBuy or iShop).

However, they can give immense value for your credit card points if you understand and learn the game, book tickets sufficiently in advance, and are not travelling in a large group. ✈️

And if you are a beginner, Air India’s Maharaja Club is one of the easiest airline loyalty programs to start with.

✅ Award tickets can be booked online for self and family without calling customer care.
✅ Very easy to check availability and miles requirement.
✅ Excellent usability across a large number of domestic as well as international routes.
✅ Air India Express flights can now also be booked using Maharaja Club Miles. 🎉

⏳ Today is the last date to transfer points from your credit cards to Maharaja Club under their 10%-50% Bonus Miles Promotion.

⚠️ Axis Bank and HSBC cards are not eligible.

📝 Registration for the offer is mandatory.

👉Offer Link: https://www.airindia.com/in/en/maharaja-club/special-offers.html

Assess your travel plans and points balance, and if it suits your requirements, you can still take advantage of this offer.

RT by @DoBaniye: 🚨 Devaluation Alert for PhonePe SBI Cardholders! 💳

➡️Starting 01 July 2026, SBI Card is slashing monthly Reward Points (RPs) and expanding exclusions for both the PURPLE and SELECT BLACK variants.

Here is a breakdown of the bad news:
📉 1. Reduced Monthly Reward Caps
The monthly earning potential for RPs is taking a hit across both cards:

• PhonePe SBI Card PURPLE:
👉 PhonePe Spends: Dropping from a flat max of 1,000 RPs to a split cap—Max 250 RPs on Insurance + Max 750 RPs on Other spends.
👉 Online Spends: Slashing from Max 1,000 RPs down to Max 750 RPs.

• PhonePe SBI Card SELECT BLACK:
👉 PhonePe Spends: Dropping from a flat max of 2,000 RPs to a split cap—Max 500 RPs on Insurance + Max 1,500 RPs on Other spends.
👉 Online Spends: Chopped straight in half from Max 2,000 RPs down to Max 1,000 RPs.

❌ 2. Massive Reward Exclusions
Say goodbye to earning points on several everyday spends. Rewards will no longer accrue on:
• Tolls and Bridges (MCC 4784) 🗺️
• Gifts and Souvenirs (MCC 5947) 🎁
• Jewellery (MCC 5094, 5944) 💎
• Educational Institutions (MCC 8220) 🎓

• Non-PhonePe App Spends: Utilities, Insurance, and UPI transactions made outside the PhonePe app will earn zero rewards. 📲
Time to recalibrate your wallet strategy before July! 💳📉

SBI cashback card is still the king 👑 - https://bitli.in/7t731vp

RT by @spendwiselyx: 💳✨ One of the best things about all Amex credit cards is that almost every type of spend counts towards milestone benefits. And that makes Amex cards especially useful for categories where many other cards give little or no rewards — like ⛽ fuel, 🏛️ government payments, 🛡️ insurance, 💰 NPS contributions etc.

👉 The Amex Reserve Credit Card offers a monthly milestone benefit where you get a ₹1,000 gift card (choose from BigBasket 🛒, Tata CliQ 🛍️, MakeMyTrip ✈️, PVR 🎬, BookMyShow 🍿) on ₹50,000 monthly spend.

And the best part? Even here, there are no spend exclusions for milestone qualification. 👏

Here’s how the rewards stack up 👇

🔹 Base rewards:
1 Reward Point per ₹50 spend = 2 Points per ₹100 spend
Assuming 1 point = ₹0.50 value ➝ approx 1% valueback 💰

🔹 Monthly milestone:
₹1,000 gift card on ₹50,000 spend ➝ Additional 2% valueback 🎁

➡️ Total = Approx 3% valueback on regular spending

🔹 Use Amex Multiplier portal:
Buy gift cards of popular brands through Amex Multiplier and earn 2X additional points 🚀

➡️ Extra ~2% valueback
➡️ Total becomes up to 5% valueback 🔥

So effectively:

⛽ Fuel, Insurance, Government Payments, NPS & Utilities
✅ No base rewards but eligible for milestone benefits
➡️ Approx 2% valueback through milestone rewards

🛒 Regular spends
✅ 1% base rewards + 2% milestone
➡️ Approx 3% valueback

🎯 Amex Multiplier spends
✅ 1% base + 2% milestone + 2% extra
➡️ Approx 5% valueback

Apart from rewards, Amex Reserve also includes premium lifestyle benefits ✨

🎁 Accor Explorer Membership every year
🍽️ Taj Epicure Membership every year
🥂 EazyDiner Prime Membership every year
🛫 12 Domestic Lounge Accesses/year (2 per quarter)
🌍 2 International Lounge Accesses/year + Free Priority Pass
⛳ Complimentary Golf Games every month (subject to ₹50,000 spend in previous month)

🎉 Plus, if you apply through referral on or before 31st May, you can get 19,000 Amex Membership Rewards points (worth approx ₹9,500 or potentially much higher depending on redemption) 🎁

🔗 Referral link (if interested in applying):
👉 https://bit.ly/46eCjfc

If you frequently stay at Accor 🏨 or Taj 🏨, this card may be a useful proposition, considering the joining benefits, annual memberships and other premium lifestyle benefits. ✨
